evals.prompt: I need to deploy DeepSeek-R1 (a MoE model) with data parallelism on MIF using Odin. I have AMD MI300X GPUs with 8 GPUs per node. I want data parallel size 16 with dataLocal 8, expert parallelism enabled, and tensor parallelism 8. The model should be served in prefill-only mode for a PD-disaggregated setup. Create both a custom InferenceServiceTemplate preset and the InferenceService that uses it, with Heimdall integration.
evals.expected_output: An InferenceServiceTemplate with spec.model.name, parallelism (data: 16, dataLocal: 8, expert: true), workerTemplate (not template) with container name 'main', GPU resources, and node selectors. Plus an InferenceService using templateRefs [vllm-prefill-dp, custom-preset], parallelism.tensor: 8, workerTemplate override for HF_TOKEN, and inferencePoolRefs to heimdall.
assertions: InferenceServiceTemplate uses apiVersion: odin.moreh.io/v1alpha1 and kind: InferenceServiceTemplate
Template uses workerTemplate (not template) since it's a *-dp runtime-base pattern
Template container name is 'main' (required for strategic merge patch)
Template includes parallelism with data: 16, dataLocal: 8, and expert: true
InferenceService uses apiVersion: odin.moreh.io/v1alpha1 and kind: InferenceService
InferenceService templateRefs includes a vllm-prefill-dp runtime-base (not vllm or vllm-dp)
InferenceService sets parallelism.tensor: 8
InferenceService has inferencePoolRefs with name: heimdall
GPU resources specified as amd.com/gpu (not nvidia.com/gpu)
Node selector uses moai.moreh.io/accelerator labels for AMD MI300X

evals.prompt: I have a model that's too large to fit on a single node with 8 GPUs. I need to split it across 4 nodes using pipeline parallelism with tensor parallelism of 8 per node. Which Odin runtime-base should I use, what does the InferenceService spec look like, and what is the resulting workload topology? Also explain why I can't use data parallelism together with pipeline parallelism.
evals.expected_output: Use vllm-pp runtime-base. InferenceService with parallelism.pipeline: 4, parallelism.tensor: 8, workerTemplate (not template). Results in a LeaderWorkerSet with 4 workers per group. Pipeline and data are mutually exclusive (enforced by validating webhook).
assertions: Recommends vllm-pp (or vllm-decode-pp/vllm-prefill-pp) as the runtime-base
Sets parallelism.pipeline: 4 for 4 pipeline stages across 4 nodes
Sets parallelism.tensor: 8 for 8 GPUs per node
Uses workerTemplate (not template) since *-pp is a LeaderWorkerSet runtime-base
Explains that the workload type is LeaderWorkerSet with 4 workers per group
Explains that pipeline and data parallelism are mutually exclusive (enforced by validating webhook)
Mentions that LeaderWorkerSet uses LeaderCreatedStartupPolicy (leader starts first)

evals.prompt: I deployed an InferenceService with templateRefs [vllm-dp, my-preset] and set env vars in spec.template.spec.containers, but my env overrides are being ignored. The pods are using the default values from the preset instead. What's going wrong and how do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: The issue is using spec.template instead of spec.workerTemplate. The vllm-dp runtime-base defines its pod spec in workerTemplate, so overrides must go in spec.workerTemplate to match the merge target. Using spec.template creates a separate unused field that doesn't merge with the runtime-base.
assertions: Identifies the root cause: using spec.template instead of spec.workerTemplate for a *-dp runtime-base
Explains that vllm-dp defines its pod spec in workerTemplate, not template
Explains the fix: move overrides from spec.template to spec.workerTemplate
Mentions that templates merge by container name (strategic merge patch with 'name' as merge key)
Mentions that the container name must be 'main' to match the runtime-base
Explains the templateRefs merge order (left-to-right, InferenceService spec applied last)
